original_abstract:
The difunctionalization of internal alkenes via one- or two-electron pathways offers a direct route to molecular complexity by establishing two contiguous $\text{Csp}^3$ stereocenters. While racemic methods have been widely explored, asymmetric versions—particularly those involving open-shell carbon-centered radicals—remain scarce in both number and scope. In this work, we present enantioenriched arylsulfinylamides as "all-in-one" reagents for the efficient, intermolecular asymmetric aminoarylation of alkenes. 

Under mild photoredox conditions, these reagents undergo a nitrogen-centered addition to the double bond followed by a 1,4-translocation of the aromatic ring. This single-operation cascade yields aminoarylation adducts with high enantiomeric purity. Notably, the sulfinyl group functions as a traceless chiral auxiliary, as it is eliminated *in situ* under the reaction conditions. This methodology provides streamlined access to optically pure $\beta,\beta$-diarylethylamines, aryl-$\alpha,\beta$-ethylenediamines, and $\alpha$-aryl-$\beta$-aminoalcohols—structural motifs frequently found in pharmaceuticals, natural products, and transition-metal ligands. The process is characterized by exceptional levels of regio-, relative, and absolute stereocontrol across a broad range of substrates.
humanized_abstract:
The difunctionalization of internal alkenes via one- or two-electron pathways is an efficient method to achieve complex structures through two carbon centers bearing two $\text{Csp}^3$ bonds each. While racemic strategy was extensively studied, the asymmetric approach particularly the open-shell carbon-centered radical variants remain limited in both the number of examples and the extent of functional diversification. Herein, we report the use of enantioenriched arylsulfinylamides as all-in-one reagents enabling intermolecular asymmetric aminoarylation of alkenes.

Under mild photoredox conditions, these reagents undergo a nitrogen-centered addition to the double bond, which is subsequently followed by a 1,4-translocation of the aromatic ring in a single-operation cascade to form aminoarylation adducts with high enantiomeric purity. The sulfinyl group in the starting materials acts as a traceless chiral auxiliary that is eliminated *in situ* under the reaction conditions. This protocol allows for an efficient synthesis of the economically and scientifically significant class of optically pure $\beta,\beta$-diarylethylamines, aryl-$\alpha,\beta$-ethylenediamines, and $\alpha$-aryl-$\beta$-aminoalcohols that are common to many drugs, natural products, and transition-metal ligands. Importantly, all these products are obtained with exceptional levels of regioselectivity, stereochemistry, and diastereocontrol across a wide range of distinct substrates.
